mission.title: porter: Proposal: Naming/Versioning Manifest updates
mission.description: Proposal: Naming/Versioning Manifest updates. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current porter deployment
steps.description:
Verify your porter version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n porter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=porter
helm list -n porter 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working porter installation.

steps.title: Review porter configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant porter configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n porter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=porter
kubectl get configmap -n porter -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=porter
```
# Proposal: Naming/Versioning Manifest updates

This issue proposes to update the naming and versioning fields in the Porter manifest, as well as prescribe some immediate follow-ups around Porter build and publish functionality to utilize the

steps.title: Apply the fix for Proposal: Naming/Versioning Manifest updates
steps.description:
# What does this change
Based on feedback provided in Issue #1082, I've started some changed to add/update the porter documentation. 

I've added a porter-yaml.md page which I think of as a page 2 for the quickstart. It's not linked in yet, I wanted to make sure to get feedback (maybe updating
```yaml
name: mybuns
version: 0.1.0
registry: myregistry.com/myorg
# Optional: can use reference to specify the exact bundle reference (correlate to deprecated `tag` field)
# reference: myregistry.com/myorg/mybuns:v0.1.0
```

steps.title: Upgrade porter to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade porter:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade porter porter/porter --namespace porter
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n porter
helm list -n porter
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n porter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=porter
kubectl get events -n porter --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Proposal: Naming/Versioning Manifest updates" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
# What does this change
Based on feedback provided in Issue #1082, I've started some changed to add/update the porter documentation. 

I've added a porter-yaml.md page which I think of as a page 2 for the quickstart.
codeSnippets:
name: mybuns
version: 0.1.0
registry: myregistry.com/myorg
# Optional: can use reference to specify the exact bundle reference (correlate to deprecated `tag` field)
# reference: myregistry.com/myorg/mybuns:v0.1.0

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with porter installed or the issue environment reproducible.
